learning_objectives.text: Define global public health and distinguish it from traditional public health practice.
Explain the historical evolution of global public health from international sanitary conferences to contemporary global health governance.
Apply core principles of global public health (equity, human rights, evidence-based practice) to analyze contemporary health challenges.
Analyze the roles and relationships of key global health actors including WHO, multilateral organizations, and civil society.
Evaluate the effectiveness of global health initiatives using case studies from different WHO regions and income levels.
Synthesize knowledge of global health principles to propose solutions for emerging health challenges that transcend national boundaries.

case_studies.title: Smallpox Eradication: A Global Public Health Success
key_lessons: Demonstrates power of coordinated global action and surveillance

case_studies.title: Rwanda's Health System Transformation (1994-2024)
key_lessons: Post-conflict health system rebuilding and community-based care

case_studies.title: Thailand's Universal Health Coverage Journey
key_lessons: Policy innovation in achieving universal health coverage
